 {"id":"bd-217r","title":"Workstream: Interactive Rendering Parity (header, tool/thinking toggles)","description":"# Goal
Improve interactive rendering to match legacy Pi’s UX:
- Startup header with shortcut hints and loaded resources
- Optional changelog display on upgrade
- Proper rendering of tool calls/results as first-class chat items
- Collapse/expand tool output
- Hide/show thinking blocks (setting + toggle)

# Legacy UX
Legacy interactive layout includes:
- Startup header with keybinding hints derived from config.
- Chat messages showing tool calls and results (collapsible via Ctrl+O).
- Thinking blocks visible/collapsible (Ctrl+T) and hideThinkingBlock setting.

Legacy references:
- `legacy_pi_mono_code/pi-mono/packages/coding-agent/README.md` (Interactive Mode)
- `legacy_pi_mono_code/pi-mono/packages/coding-agent/docs/settings.md` (hideThinkingBlock, quietStartup, collapseChangelog)
- `legacy_pi_mono_code/pi-mono/packages/coding-agent/docs/tui.md`

# Current Rust State (Gap)
- Header is minimal (just “Pi (model)”).
- Thinking blocks are always rendered (truncated) with no hide/toggle.
- Tool calls/results are not modeled as distinct UI items, so collapse/expand is not possible.

# Scope / Deliverables
1) Startup header:
- Show logo + version.
- Show keybinding hints (requires keybindings workstream).
- Show loaded resources summary (skills/prompts/themes/extensions).
- Respect `quiet_startup` and `collapse_changelog`.

2) Tool call/result rendering:
- Represent tool calls/results as distinct message types in the conversation model.
- Add collapse state (global or per-message).

3) Thinking visibility:
- Implement `hide_thinking_block` setting.
- Implement toggle action (`toggleThinking`) to hide/show.

4) Tool output toggle:
- Implement `expandTools` action to collapse/expand tool output.

# Testing
- Snapshot/state tests for:
  - header rendering in quiet/non-quiet
  - collapse toggles

# Dependencies
- Keybindings parity (`bd-3ip`).

## Acceptance Criteria
[ ] Startup header shows keybinding hints and loaded resources
[ ] quietStartup and collapseChangelog are respected
[ ] Tool calls/results rendered as structured chat items
[ ] Ctrl+O toggles tool output visibility
[ ] hideThinkingBlock respected and Ctrl+T toggles thinking
","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Startup header shows keybinding hints and loaded resources

[ ] Ctrl+O toggles tool output visibility
[ ] hideThinkingBlock respected and Ctrl+T toggles thinking","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:51:51.357278208Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T20:00:10.195394089Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-217r","depends_on_id":"bd-2qk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-21gp","title":"Interactive: Ctrl+P model cycling (scoped)","description":"# Goal
Implement legacy scoped model cycling:
- `Ctrl+P` cycle forward
- `Shift+Ctrl+P` cycle backward

# Scope
- Determine cycling list:
  - if scoped patterns are set → use filtered list
  - else use all available models
- Stable ordering to avoid surprising jumps.

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Cycling changes the active model and persists to session header.
- [ ] Cycling respects scoped models.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:45:58.983843582Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T19:46:33.575333365Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-21gp","depends_on_id":"bd-1jdk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:00Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-21gp","depends_on_id":"bd-gze","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:00Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-22h","title":"Design stratified sampling matrix for extension diversity","description":"Background:
- The sample must cover extension feature variety, not just popularity.

Include axes such as:
- Capabilities used: read/write/exec/http/env.
- Interaction model: tool-only vs slash commands vs event hooks.
- Runtime tier: WASM vs JS bundle vs MCP/process.
- Complexity: single-file vs multi-module; config-heavy vs config-light.
- I/O patterns: filesystem-heavy, network-heavy, CPU-heavy.

Output:
- A sampling matrix/table that maps each candidate extension to axes, with quotas per axis.

Acceptance:
- Matrix makes it explicit why each selected extension is in the sample.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:21:15.663936275Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T05:47:39.873324254Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T05:47:39.873260104Z","close_reason":"Stratified sampling matrix documented in docs/EXTENSION_SAMPLING_MATRIX.md","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-22h","depends_on_id":"bd-2uv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:02Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-22h","depends_on_id":"bd-gx1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:02Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":147,"issue_id":"bd-22h","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Added docs/EXTENSION_SAMPLING_MATRIX.md with axis quotas (target=16) and full candidate tag mapping using docs/EXTENSION_CANDIDATES.md + CONFORMANCE.md criteria.","created_at":"2026-02-03T05:47:28Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-22k8","title":"Release: add CHANGELOG.md (generated from closed beads)","description":"# Goal
Add a first-class `CHANGELOG.md` to the repo and define how it is generated/maintained.

# Background
This repo already tracks work granularly via beads. `br changelog` can generate a changelog from closed issues, but we need a stable, user-facing file committed to git.

# Requirements
- `CHANGELOG.md` must be readable without beads context.
- Entries should be grouped by type (feature/task/bug/chore) and/or by release version.
- The file should be regenerated in a deterministic way (same input → same output), or at least documented if manual edits are expected.

# Proposed Workflow
1) Run `br changelog` and capture output.
2) Post-process minimally (if needed): add headings, link issue IDs, remove noise.
3) Commit `CHANGELOG.md` updates as part of the release cut.

# Automation (optional but recommended)
- Add a CI job or a `just`/`make`-style documented command that regenerates changelog output.
- If we publish GitHub Releases, use the same content for release notes.

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `CHANGELOG.md` exists at repo root.
- [ ] `br changelog` output mapping is documented (in `docs/releasing.md`).
- [ ] A human can update the changelog during a release with one copy/paste step.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T21:21:37.775978210Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T21:21:37.799165877Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-22k8","depends_on_id":"bd-gqtd","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"}]}
+{"id":"bd-22k8","title":"Release: add CHANGELOG.md (generated from closed beads)","description":"# Goal
Add a first-class `CHANGELOG.md` to the repo and define how it is generated/maintained.

# Background
This repo already tracks work granularly via beads. `br changelog` can generate a changelog from closed issues, but we need a stable, user-facing file committed to git.

# Requirements
- `CHANGELOG.md` must be readable without beads context.
- Entries should be grouped by type (feature/task/bug/chore) and/or by release version.
- The file should be regenerated in a deterministic way (same input → same output), or at least documented if manual edits are expected.

# Proposed Workflow
1) Run `br changelog` and capture output.
2) Post-process minimally (if needed): add headings, link issue IDs, remove noise.
3) Commit `CHANGELOG.md` updates as part of the release cut.

# Automation (optional but recommended)
- Add a CI job or a `just`/`make`-style documented command that regenerates changelog output.
- If we publish GitHub Releases, use the same content for release notes.

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `CHANGELOG.md` exists at repo root.
- [ ] `br changelog` output mapping is documented (in `docs/releasing.md`).
- [ ] A human can update the changelog during a release with one copy/paste step.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T21:21:37.775978210Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T00:33:59.407762669Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T00:33:59.407685545Z","close_reason":"Added repo-root CHANGELOG.md + documented br changelog workflow (commit b4f703a)","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-22k8","depends_on_id":"bd-gqtd","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-22p","title":"Workstream: Theme System Implementation","description":"# Workstream: Theme System Implementation

## Goal
Implement theme loading and switching for the TUI, matching Pi Agent's theme system.

## Background
The TypeScript Pi Agent supports themes that control:
- Color palette (foreground, background, accent colors)
- Syntax highlighting colors
- Status line appearance
- Thinking block styling

Themes are stored as JSON files in:
- ~/.pi/agent/themes/ (global)
- .pi/themes/ (project)
- Package themes (installed via pi install)

## Scope

### In Scope
1. Theme file format definition (JSON schema)
2. Theme discovery from standard locations
3. Theme application to TUI components
4. Theme switching via /theme command
5. Default themes (dark, light, solarized)

### Out of Scope
1. Theme creation wizard
2. Real-time theme editing
3. Complex inheritance chains

## Theme Format
```json
{
  "name": "ocean-dark",
  "version": "1.0",
  "colors": {
    "foreground": "#d4d4d4",
    "background": "#1e1e1e",
    "accent": "#007acc",
    "success": "#4ec9b0",
    "warning": "#ce9178",
    "error": "#f44747",
    "muted": "#6a6a6a"
  },
  "syntax": {
    "keyword": "#569cd6",
    "string": "#ce9178",
    "number": "#b5cea8",
    "comment": "#6a9955",
    "function": "#dcdcaa"
  },
  "ui": {
    "border": "#3c3c3c",
    "selection": "#264f78",
    "cursor": "#aeafad"
  }
}
```

## Implementation Phases

### Phase 1: Theme Loading
- Parse theme JSON files
- Discover themes from standard locations
- Validate theme format
- Provide default themes

### Phase 2: Theme Application
- Apply colors to lipgloss styles
- Wire syntax colors to glamour
- Update all TUI components

### Phase 3: Theme Switching
- /theme command to list/switch
- Persist theme preference in settings
- Hot-reload on theme file change (optional)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Theme files parsed correctly
- [ ] Default themes work
- [ ] /theme command works
- [ ] TUI renders with theme colors
- [ ] Settings remembers theme preference

## Files to Create
- src/theme.rs (theme loading/application)
- themes/dark.json (default dark theme)
- themes/light.json (default light theme)

## Files to Modify
- src/interactive.rs (apply theme to TUI)
- src/tui.rs (apply theme to console output)
- src/config.rs (theme preference setting)","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:39:16.349318511Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T03:39:16.356035177Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-22p","depends_on_id":"bd-2qk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:00Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-247","title":"Fix asupersync websocket unused import warning","description":"asupersync emits unused imports warning in net/websocket/client.rs during builds.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T01:26:23.304039205Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T01:52:45.712771158Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T01:52:45.712697421Z","close_reason":"Fixed unused import warning and clippy clean in asupersync","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-24f","title":"Unit tests: PackageManager path resolution + filters","description":"Goal:
- Add unit tests for PackageManager path resolution, package identity parsing, and resource filters using real temp dirs, with detailed logging.

Scope:
- parse_source for npm/git/local and identity normalization.
- Installed path resolution for user/project scopes.
- Resource filters: extensions/skills/prompts/themes enable/disable behavior.
- Deterministic resolution ordering and error paths.

Logging Requirements:
- Use TestHarness/TestLogger (bd-3ml) in each test.
- Log parsed source components, resolved paths, and filter decisions.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Tests use real temp dirs and filesystem paths.
- No mocks; no network access.
- Edge cases covered (pinned refs, invalid sources, missing dirs).
- Logs include inputs + resolved outputs for debugging.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T04:58:46.313157289Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T09:16:34.775048154Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T09:16:34.774969117Z","close_reason":"Added deterministic PackageManager tests (identity/installed_path/filters) + ResolveRoots injection","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-24f","depends_on_id":"bd-26s","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-24f","depends_on_id":"bd-3ml","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}
